steps.title: Substitute using the repeated expression
steps.content:
Notice that $(x-4)$ appears on both sides of the equation. Let $u = x - 4$ to reduce complexity:
$$6(x-4) = (x-4) + 6$$
becomes
$$6u = u + 6$$

steps.title: Solve for u to find x - 4
steps.content:
Subtract $u$ from both sides:
$$6u - u = 6$$
$$5u = 6$$
Divide both sides by 5:
$$u = \frac{6}{5}$$
Since $u = x - 4$, we have $x - 4 = \frac{6}{5} = 1.2$.

Equivalent forms accepted: 6/5, 1.2.
